title: thymus ciliatus subsp. coloratus
intro: Thymus ciliatus subsp. coloratus, also known as the mountain thyme, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family, Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb with small, fragrant flowers and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Mountain thyme can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to promote digestion, relieve stress, and boost the immune system.
